attacks.name: Inferno Fang
attacks.effect: You may discard all Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: Melting Horn
attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Grand Flame

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

This card provides Colorless Energy. While in play, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon.

This card can only be attached to Fire Pokémon. This card provides Fire Energy only while this card is attached to a Fire Pokémon.

If this card is discarded by an attack of the Fire Pokémon this card is attached to, attach this card from your discard pile to that Pokémon after attacking.

(If this card is attached to anything other than a Fire Pokémon, discard this card.)

Turn all of your Prize cards face up. (Those Prize cards remain face up for the rest of the game.)

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Shuffle 3 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card, shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of it.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Choose which way this card faces before you play it. This ↓ player can't have more than 3 Benched Pokémon. (When this card comes into play, this ↓ player discards Benched Pokémon until he or she has 3 Pokémon on the Bench.)

Choose which way this card faces before you play it. Any damage done by attacks from this ↓ player's Grass, Fire, or Water Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Mewtwo-EX.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws 4 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Houndoom-EX.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If the Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is 3 or more, that Pokémon gets +20 HP and can't be Confused. (If that Pokémon is currently Confused, remove that Special Condition.)

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for a Pokémon with a Retreat Cost of 3 or more, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Glalie-EX.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Choose 1:
• Draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.
• During this turn, your Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

The Pokémon this card is attached to has no Retreat Cost.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Put 4 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Each player puts a Pokémon from his or her discard pile into his or her hand. (Your opponent chooses first.)

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for 1 Basic Pokémon-EX or 3 Basic Pokémon (except for Pokémon-EX) and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Any damage done to the Pokémon this card is attached to by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that have any Special Energy attached to them is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Mysterious Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Even a robust wrestler will become dizzy and unable to stand when exposed to its 200,000-hertz ultrasonic waves.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Even a robust wrestler will become dizzy and unable to stand when exposed to its 200,000-hertz ultrasonic waves.

attacks.name: Dual Cut
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.
They will challenge anything, even strong opponents, without fear. Their frequent fights help them become stronger.

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

attacks.name: Cyclone Slash
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Brave Bird
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It lives in forests and fields. Squabbles over territory occur when flocks collide.

attacks.name: Call for Pals
attacks.effect: Search your deck for as many Starly as you like and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

attacks.name: Bind Wound
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.
Once it becomes an adult, it has a tendency to let its comrades plant footprints on its back.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Swing Around
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Although it has a large body, it is quite skilled at climbing trees. It eats and sleeps in the treetops.
Before food becomes scarce in wintertime, its habit is to hoard food in many hidden locations.

attacks.name: High Flight
attacks.effect: Each player reveals his or her hand. This attack does 20 damage times the number of Item cards revealed.

flavorText: When it needs to think, it rotates its head 180 degrees to sharpen its intellectual power.

attacks.name: Proclaim the Night
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't play any Item cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.

flavorText: It always stand on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: An odd species that is rarely found. The three heads respectively represent joy, sadness, and anger.

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Doduo Delivery
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.

attacks.name: Simultaneous Peck
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Doduo Delivery
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.

attacks.name: Exhausted Tackle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Adores round objects. It wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

attacks.name: Synchro Woofer
attacks.effect: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Noivern BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Tuning
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The ultrasonic waves it emits from its ears can reduce a large boulder to pebbles. It emerges out of the dark to attack.

attacks.name: Dragon Dance
attacks.effect: As long as this Haxorus is your Active Pokémon, each of its attacks does 100 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't add more than 100 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

flavorText: Their sturdy tusks will stay sharp even if used to cut steel beams. These Pokémon are covered in hard armor.

attacks.name: Dragon Slayer
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Dragon Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: A broken tusk will not grow back, so it diligently sharpens its tusks on river rocks after the end of a battle.

attacks.name: Extra Chop
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: They mark their territory by leaving gashes in trees with their tusks. If a tusk breaks, a new one grows in quickly.
They mark their territory by leaving gashes in trees with their tusks. If a tusk breaks, a new one grows in quickly.

attacks.name: Rainbow Force
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.

attacks.name: Power Creation
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: When the horns on its head shine in seven different colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life.

attacks.name: Heavy Perfume
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. Put 6 damage counters instead of 3 on that Pokémon for this Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hug
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its scent is so overpowering that, unless a Trainer happens to really enjoy the smell, he or she will have a hard time walking alongside it.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.

flavorText: In the past, rather than using perfume, royal ladies carried a Spritzee that would waft a fragrance they liked.

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Florges BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Wonder Shine
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: In times long past, governors of castles would invite Florges to create flower gardens to embellish the castle domains.

attacks.name: Aromatherapy
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When the flowers of a well-tended flower bed bloom, it appears and celebrates with an elegant dance.

attacks.name: Wish
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: When it finds a flower it likes, it dwells on that flower its whole life long. It floats in the wind's embrace with an untroubled heart.
It is highly attuned to the emotions of people and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

attacks.name: Startling Bark
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 3 random cards from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Tantrum
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It can make most any Pokémon run away simply by opening its mouth wide to reveal its big fangs.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Small Pokémon flee from its scary face. It is, however, considered by women to be cute.

attacks.name: Juggling
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is adept at conning people. It is said to be able to create walls out of thin air by miming.

attacks.name: Pain Amplifier
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Ancient people believed that petitioning Bronzong for rain was the way to make crops grow.

attacks.name: Payback
attacks.effect: If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Implements shaped like it were discovered in ancient tombs. It is unknown if they are related.

attacks.name: Pitch-Black Spear
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon-EX. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

attacks.name: Disorderly Flip
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It flashes the light-emitting spots on its body, which drains its opponent's will to fight. It takes the opportunity to scuttle away and hide.

attacks.name: Foul Play
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Zoroark BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

attacks.name: Whiny Voice
attacks.effect: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Dark Edge
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

attacks.name: Moonless Madness
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Dark Edge
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

attacks.name: Derail
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Packs of them follow travelers through the desert until the travelers can no longer move.

attacks.name: Skyward Kick
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against.

attacks.name: Pompous Punch
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It does its level best to glare and pull a scary face, but it can't help grinning if anyone pats its head.

attacks.name: Accelerating Spin
attacks.effect: Attach 2 Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon. Then, switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Prima Rondo
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Psychic Energy attached to it, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

attacks.name: Sensitive Blade
attacks.effect: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: A master of courtesy and swordsmanship, it fights using extending swords on its elbows.

attacks.name: Nose Jet
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It enshrouds itself with sand to protect itself from germs. It does not enjoy getting wet.

attacks.name: Primordial Boom
attacks.effect: If you have a Stadium card in play, this attack does 40 more damage. If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its impressive tusks are made of ice. The population thinned when it turned warm after the ice age.

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gathering Footsteps
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each Colorless in the Retreat Cost of your Swinub, Piloswine, and Mamoswine.

flavorText: Because the long hair all over its body obscures its sight, it just keeps charging repeatedly.

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: If it smells something enticing, it dashes off headlong to find the source of the aroma.

attacks.name: Bone Revenge
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Marowak BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Sharpshooting
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Bone Windmill
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The bone it holds is its key weapon. It throws the bone skillfully like a boomerang to KO targets.

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It always wears the skull of its dead mother, so no one has any idea what its hidden face looks like.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Curls up into a spiny ball when threatened. It can roll while curled up to attack or escape.

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Disliking water, it lives in deep burrows in arid areas. It can roll itself instantly into a ball.

attacks.name: Mind Bullet
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon times the amount of Energy attached to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It uses psychic power to control an opponent's brain and tamper with its memories.

attacks.name: Psych Up
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Psych Up attack does 20 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Rumors of its origin are linked to a UFO crash site in the desert 50 years ago.

attacks.name: Wave Amplification
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Returning Echo attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Returning Echo
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

flavorText: Anyone who comes into contact with the ultrasonic waves emitted by a courting male experiences a positive mood shift.

attacks.name: Odor Sleuth
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, put a card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: The heart-shaped mark left on a body after a woobat has been attached to it is said to bring good fortune.

attacks.name: Moonlight Gain
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Those who sleep holding Cresselia's feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

attacks.name: Beckon
attacks.effect: Put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Quick Turn
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is highly attuned to the emotions of people and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

attacks.name: Mirror Barrier
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

attacks.name: Curse Deeply
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its cries sound like incantations to torment the foe. It appears where you least expect it.

attacks.name: Haunt
attacks.effect: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It likes playing mischievous tricks, such as screaming and wailing to startle people at night.

attacks.name: Psychic Infinity
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.

attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sinister Fog
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Creep Show
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has 3 or more damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: Hiding in people's shadows at night, it absorbs their heat. The chill it causes makes the victims shake.

attacks.name: Poison Ring
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. That Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It licks with its gaseous tongue to steal the victim's life force. It lurks in darkness for prey.

attacks.name: Sleep Poison
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

flavorText: Born from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It uses its tail to absorb electricity from power plants or from outlets in houses, and then it fires the electricity from its whiskers.

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: Thunder Blast
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity.

attacks.name: Thunder Lance
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This rough Pokémon stores energy inside its body, then sweeps across the land, shooting off electricity.

attacks.name: Thunder Blast
attacks.effect: Discard a Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It evolved from exposure to a special magnetic field. Three units generate magnetism.

attacks.name: Static Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Electro Ball
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Generates strange radio signals. It raises the temperature by 3.6 degrees Fahrenheit within 3,300 feet.

attacks.name: Shock Generator
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It moves while constantly hovering. It discharges Thunder Wave and so on from the units at its sides.
It moves while constantly hovering. It discharges Thunder Wave and so on from the units at its sides.

attacks.name: Grand Bolt
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Raichu BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Thunderclap Shot
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon-EX. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Electrosmash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It can loose 100,000-volt bursts of electricity instantly downing foes several times its size.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It has small electric sacs on both its cheeks. If threatened, it looses electric charges from the sacs.
Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute's time.
It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

attacks.name: Frigid Breath
attacks.effect: Until the end of your next turn, each player can't play any Supporter or Stadium cards from his or her hand.

attacks.name: Deep Freeze
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and, when angry, cause violent blizzards.

attacks.name: Ice Edge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon's habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. It sleeps buried in snow.

attacks.name: Green Fling
attacks.effect: Put 3 Grass Energy from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Hand Fling
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage times the number of cards in your hand.

flavorText: It prefers places with clean water. When its tuft runs low, it replenishes it by siphoning up water with its tail.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

attacks.name: Ice Age
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Dragon Pokémon, it is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Double Smash
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: In the spring, it grows berries with the texture of frozen treats around its belly.
The three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. The leader has the biggest horns.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lives a solitary life. Its wings deliver wicked blows that can snap even the thickest of trees.
Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

attacks.name: Cryo Mouth
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has 10 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 150 more damage.

rules:
Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.

Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ice Breath
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Instant Freeze
attacks.effect: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 100 more damage.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hug
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It traps foes with the suction cups on its tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

attacks.name: Ion Pool
attacks.effect: Discard any Stadium card in play.

flavorText: Using its dorsal fin as a suction pad, it clings to a Mantine's underside to scavenge for leftovers.

attacks.name: Wild River
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Using its dorsal fin as a suction pad, it clings to a Mantine's underside to scavenge for leftovers.

attacks.name: Deep Sea Swirl
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 7 cards.

attacks.name: Balance Bind
attacks.effect: If you and your opponent have the same number of Benched Pokémon, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.

attacks.name: Continuous Spin
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It appears in large numbers by seashores. At night, its central core flashes with a red light.

attacks.name: Soaking Horn
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It makes its nest by hollowing out boulders in streams with its horn. It defends its eggs with its life.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its dorsal, pectoral and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the water dancer.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When the twig is plucked from its tail, friction sets the twig alight. The flame is used to send signals to its allies.

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears.

attacks.name: Water Fling
attacks.effect: Put 3 Water Energy from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Hand Fling
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage times the number of cards in your hand.

flavorText: When it gets excited, embers rise from its head and tail and it gets hot. For some reason, it loves sweets.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon lives in caves in volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.name: Inferno Fang
attacks.effect: You may discard all Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage.

rules:
Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.

Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Melting Horn
attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Grand Flame
attacks.effect: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Massive Eruption
attacks.effect: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 80 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.

attacks.name: Flare Destroy
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. Then, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur together to cause huge explosions.

attacks.name: Mini Eruption
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is an Energy card, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Be careful if it turns its back during battle. It means that it will attack with the fire on its back.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a timid nature. If it is startled, the flames on its back burn more vigorously.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Horn Leech
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: They inhabit mountainous regions. The leader of the herd is decided by a battle of clashing horns.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: If it has sunshine and water, it doesn't need to eat, because it can generate energy from the leaves on its back.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The patterns on this Pokémon's wings depend on the climate and topography of its habitat. It scatters colorful scales.

attacks.name: Protect
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: The beaks of birds can't begin to scratch its stalwart body. To defend itself, it spews powder.

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate.

attacks.name: Tough Hammer
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself. This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Chesnaught BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Spike Lariat
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Adamantine Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: When it takes a defensive posture with its fists guarding its face, it could withstand a bomb blast.

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: They strengthen their lower bodies by running into one another. They are very kind and won't start fights.

attacks.name: Tree Climb
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.
Such a thick shell of wood covers its head and back that even a direct hit from a truck wouldn't faze it.

attacks.name: Nosh
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Such a thick shell of wood covers its head and back that even a direct hit from a truck wouldn't faze it.

attacks.name: Fire Fling
attacks.effect: Put 3 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Hand Fling
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage times the number of cards in your hand.

flavorText: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It's good at finding berries and gathers them from all over. It's kind enough to share them with friends.

attacks.name: Swagger
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It prefers harsh environments such as deserts. It can survive for 30 days on water stored in its body.

attacks.name: Overhead Throw
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Heavy Suplex
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: It swings its long antlers wildly to attack. During cold periods, it hides deep in forests.

attacks.name: Colorful Spores
attacks.effect: Choose 3 of your Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a different type of basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The larger the mushroom on its back grows, the stronger the mushroom spores it scatters.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Burrows under the ground to gnaw on tree roots. The mushrooms on its back absorb most of the nutrition.
